summary: Completed full E2E testing of Sit Happens CRM. 20/20 backend pytest cases pass (auth, clients, portal accounts, dogs, training logs, bookings with rabies/capacity/credit validation, approve/cancel-refund, availability, dashboard stats, events, portal/me, authorization). Frontend admin login, dashboard, sidebar navigation (Dashboard/Schedule/Bookings/Clients/Dogs), Add Client modal, and client Register form all render correctly with dark theme.

issues: Review request mentions 'Calendar' label but UI uses 'Schedule' - not a bug, just naming mismatch with spec

critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 612 lines - acceptable but approaching the 700-line guideline; consider splitting into routers (auth/clients/dogs/bookings) for maintainability.
POST /api/bookings as admin auto-approves but does NOT verify admin's selected dog's owner credits when role=admin; this is by design (admin bypass) but should be documented.
CORS allow_origins defaults to '*' with allow_credentials=True - browsers reject credentialed requests with wildcard; set explicit origin in production.
Booking cost field is stored but not exposed via BookingOut model - clients can't see how many credits a booking will consume in /api/bookings list response.
_booking_days_count() loads up to 2000 bookings into memory and iterates per request - O(N) per availability call; will not scale. Add a date index or precompute.
Capacity check only runs for 'daycare', boarding has no capacity enforcement - intentional? Consider documenting.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@sithappens.com / admin123 (verified). Portal users are created dynamically per test via /api/clients/{id}/portal-account.
seed_data_creation: Tests auto-create TEST_-prefixed clients/dogs and cleanup at session teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend suite at /app/backend/tests/test_sithappens.py covers all endpoints end-to-end. Re-run with: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=... pytest backend/tests/test_sithappens.py -v. Admin auto-seeded on startup. Frontend has data-testid on login-email-input/login-password-input/login-submit-button; deeper screens (Clients/Dogs/Portal) lack data-testid on add buttons - text-based selectors used instead.
